author: m.kalinin
content: Once we have tests with different states of the system at the end, would it be helpful if clients will attempt to build a block after each test is finished and import that block to check it is correct wrt state_transition impl?

author: alex.vlasov
content:
Hey, I've generated fresh gloas F/C test vectors https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1WxbkVMYanvhPlJD6mpcvQKR98HbsZMQa
They are not fancy yet, just the current fork_choice compliance tests generated for gloas (I had also to skip block tree weights output temporarily).
We'll be updating as we improve gloas specific coverage.

author: alex.vlasov
content:
I think this is a very good idea!
It will require certain efforts on the client side, but simulating validator activity at each (or some) protocol state(s) should be a pretty tough test oracle!

author: potuz
content: It wouldn't help much on Prysm at least. It wouldn't be realistic. Warming of caches and communication with the engine is time sensitive and on spectests this is just simulated.
